work_unit: ESE-M02: autonomous-loop.sh per-round timeout + doc correction
situation: audit-2026-07-03-006 finding ESE-M02 (pending): autonomous-loop.sh header line 14 claims '--max-iterations' bounds each round, but cloudcli claude does not support that flag (line 144 self-admits). run_round() invoked claude -p with no timeout wrapper → single-round hang risk unmitigated.
action: 1) Rewrote header comment (line 14-15) to accurately describe the real bounding mechanism: timeout wrapper around claude -p, default 1800s, overridable via ROUND_TIMEOUT_SECS env var; explicitly notes cloudcli claude lacks --max-iterations. 2) Added ROUND_TIMEOUT_SECS variable (default 1800) above run_round(). 3) Wrapped claude -p invocation in run_round() with `timeout $ROUND_TIMEOUT_SECS`. Timeout exit code 124 flows through existing `|| true` + rate-limit grep logic → treated as round failure, triggering fallback/retry path naturally.
outcome_evidence: Worktree opt-autonomous-loop-shift-1783089654 committed and pushed to origin/auto/opt-autonomous-loop-shift-1783089654. Verified fix present in worktree: line 14 updated comment, line 115 ROUND_TIMEOUT_SECS var, line 122 timeout wrapper.
